english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Romans

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid ergo dicemus invenisse Abraham patrem nostrum secundum carnem?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τί οὖν ἐροῦμεν ἀβραὰμ τὸν πατέρα ἡμῶν εὑρηκέναι κατὰ σάρκα
verses.king_james: What shall we say then that Abraham our father, as pertaining to the flesh, hath found?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si enim Abraham ex operibus justificatus est, habet gloriam, sed non apud Deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ γὰρ ἀβραὰμ ἐξ ἔργων ἐδικαιώθη ἔχει καύχημα ἀλλ᾽ οὐ πρὸς τὸν θεόν
verses.king_james: For if Abraham were justified by works, he hath *whereof* to glory; but not before God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid enim dicit Scriptura? Credidit Abraham Deo, et reputatam est illi ad justitiam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τί γὰρ ἡ γραφὴ λέγει ἐπίστευσεν δὲ ἀβραὰμ τῷ θεῷ καὶ ἐλογίσθη αὐτῷ εἰς δικαιοσύνην
verses.king_james: For what saith the scripture? Abraham believed God, and it was counted unto him for righteousness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ei autem qui operatur, merces non imputatur secundum gratiam, sed secundum debitum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῷ δὲ ἐργαζομένῳ ὁ μισθὸς οὐ λογίζεται κατὰ χάριν ἀλλὰ κατὰ τό ὀφείλημα
verses.king_james: Now to him that worketh is the reward not reckoned of grace, but of debt.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ei vero qui non operatur, credenti autem in eum, qui justificat impium, reputatur fides ejus ad justitiam secundum propositum gratiæ Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῷ δὲ μὴ ἐργαζομένῳ πιστεύοντι δὲ ἐπὶ τὸν δικαιοῦντα τὸν ἀσεβῆ λογίζεται ἡ πίστις αὐτοῦ εἰς δικαιοσύνην
verses.king_james: But to him that worketh not, but believeth on him that justifieth the ungodly, his faith is counted for righteousness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut et David dicit beatitudinem hominis, cui Deus accepto fert justitiam sine operibus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθάπερ καὶ δαβὶδ λέγει τὸν μακαρισμὸν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ᾧ ὁ θεὸς λογίζεται δικαιοσύνην χωρὶς ἔργων
verses.king_james: Even as David also describeth the blessedness of the man, unto whom God imputeth righteousness without works,

verses.latin_vulgate: [Beati, quorum remissæ sunt iniquitates, et quorum tecta sunt peccata.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μακάριοι ὧν ἀφέθησαν αἱ ἀνομίαι καὶ ὧν ἐπεκαλύφθησαν αἱ ἁμαρτίαι
verses.king_james: *Saying*, Blessed *are* they whose iniquities are forgiven, and whose sins are covered.

verses.latin_vulgate: Beatus vir, cui non imputavit Dominus peccatum.]
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μακάριος ἀνὴρ ᾧ οὗ μὴ λογίσηται κύριος ἁμαρτίαν
verses.king_james: Blessed *is* the man to whom the Lord will not impute sin.

verses.latin_vulgate: Beatitudo ergo hæc in circumcisione tantum manet, an etiam in præputio? Dicimus enim quia reputata est Abrahæ fides ad justitiam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ μακαρισμὸς οὖν οὗτος ἐπὶ τὴν περιτομὴν ἢ καὶ ἐπὶ τὴν ἀκροβυστίαν λέγομεν γάρ ὅτι ἐλογίσθη τῷ ἀβραὰμ ἡ πίστις εἰς δικαιοσύνην
verses.king_james: *Cometh* this blessedness then upon the circumcision *only*, or upon the uncircumcision also? for we say that faith was reckoned to Abraham for righteousness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quomodo ergo reputata est? in circumcisione, an in præputio? Non in circumcisione, sed in præputio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πῶς οὖν ἐλογίσθη ἐν περιτομῇ ὄντι ἢ ἐν ἀκροβυστίᾳ οὐκ ἐν περιτομῇ ἀλλ᾽ ἐν ἀκροβυστίᾳ
verses.king_james: How was it then reckoned? when he was in circumcision, or in uncircumcision? Not in circumcision, but in uncircumcision.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et signum accepit circumcisionis, signaculum justitiæ fidei, quæ est in præputio: ut sit pater omnium credentium per præputium, ut reputetur et illis ad justitiam:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ σημεῖον ἔλαβεν περιτομῆς σφραγῖδα τῆς δικαιοσύνης τῆς πίστεως τῆς ἐν τῇ ἀκροβυστίᾳ εἰς τὸ εἶναι αὐτὸν πατέρα πάντων τῶν πιστευόντων δι᾽ ἀκροβυστίας εἰς τὸ λογισθῆναι καὶ αὐτοῖς τὴν δικαιοσύνην
verses.king_james: And he received the sign of circumcision, a seal of the righteousness of the faith which *he had yet* being uncircumcised: that he might be the father of all them that believe, though they be not circumcised; that righteousness might be imputed unto them also:

verses.latin_vulgate: et sit pater circumcisionis non iis tantum, qui sunt ex circumcisione, sed et iis qui sectantur vestigia fidei, quæ est in præputio patris nostri Abrahæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πατέρα περιτομῆς τοῖς οὐκ ἐκ περιτομῆς μόνον ἀλλὰ καὶ τοῖς στοιχοῦσιν τοῖς ἴχνεσιν τῆς ἐν τῇ ἀκροβυστίᾳ πίστεως τοῦ πατρὸς ἡμῶν ἀβραάμ
verses.king_james: And the father of circumcision to them who are not of the circumcision only, but who also walk in the steps of that faith of our father Abraham, which *he had* being *yet* uncircumcised.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim per legem promissio Abrahæ, aut semini ejus ut hæres esset mundi: sed per justitiam fidei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γὰρ διὰ νόμου ἡ ἐπαγγελία τῷ ἀβραὰμ ἢ τῷ σπέρματι αὐτοῦ τὸ κληρονόμον αὐτὸν εἶναι τοῦ κόσμου ἀλλὰ διὰ δικαιοσύνης πίστεως
verses.king_james: For the promise, that he should be the heir of the world, *was* not to Abraham, or to his seed, through the law, but through the righteousness of faith.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si enim qui ex lege, hæredes sunt: exinanita est fides, abolita est promissio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ γὰρ οἱ ἐκ νόμου κληρονόμοι κεκένωται ἡ πίστις καὶ κατήργηται ἡ ἐπαγγελία
verses.king_james: For if they which are of the law *be* heirs, faith is made void, and the promise made of none effect:

verses.latin_vulgate: Lex enim iram operatur. Ubi enim non est lex, nec prævaricatio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ γὰρ νόμος ὀργὴν κατεργάζεται οὗ γὰρ οὐκ ἔστιν νόμος οὐδὲ παράβασις
verses.king_james: Because the law worketh wrath: for where no law is, *there is* no transgression.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ideo ex fide, ut secundum gratiam firma sit promissio omni semini, non ei qui ex lege est solum, sed et ei qui ex fide est Abrahæ, qui pater est omnium nostrum
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ τοῦτο ἐκ πίστεως ἵνα κατὰ χάριν εἰς τὸ εἶναι βεβαίαν τὴν ἐπαγγελίαν παντὶ τῷ σπέρματι οὐ τῷ ἐκ τοῦ νόμου μόνον ἀλλὰ καὶ τῷ ἐκ πίστεως ἀβραάμ ὅς ἐστιν πατὴρ πάντων ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: Therefore *it is* of faith, that *it might be* by grace; to the end the promise might be sure to all the seed; not to that only which is of the law, but to that also which is of the faith of Abraham; who is the father of us all,

verses.latin_vulgate: (sicut scriptum est: Quia patrem multarum gentium posui te) ante Deum, cui credidit, qui vivificat mortuos, et vocat ea quæ non sunt, tamquam ea quæ sunt:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθὼς γέγραπται ὅτι πατέρα πολλῶν ἐθνῶν τέθεικά σε κατέναντι οὗ ἐπίστευσεν θεοῦ τοῦ ζῳοποιοῦντος τοὺς νεκροὺς καὶ καλοῦντος τὰ μὴ ὄντα ὡς ὄντα
verses.king_james: (As it is written, I have made thee a father of many nations,) before him whom he believed, *even* God, who quickeneth the dead, and calleth those things which be not as though they were.

verses.latin_vulgate: qui contra spem in spem credidit, ut fieret pater multarum gentium secundum quod dictum est ei: Sic erit semen tuum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς παρ᾽ ἐλπίδα ἐπ᾽ ἐλπίδι ἐπίστευσεν εἰς τὸ γενέσθαι αὐτὸν πατέρα πολλῶν ἐθνῶν κατὰ τὸ εἰρημένον οὕτως ἔσται τὸ σπέρμα σου
verses.king_james: Who against hope believed in hope, that he might become the father of many nations, according to that which was spoken, So shall thy seed be.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et non infirmatus est fide, nec consideravit corpus suum emortuum, cum jam fere centum esset annorum, et emortuam vulvam Saræ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ μὴ ἀσθενήσας τῇ πίστει οὐ κατενόησεν τὸ ἑαυτοῦ σῶμα ἤδη νενεκρωμένον ἑκατονταετής που ὑπάρχων καὶ τὴν νέκρωσιν τῆς μήτρας σάῤῥας
verses.king_james: And being not weak in faith, he considered not his own body now dead, when he was about an hundred years old, neither yet the deadness of Sara's womb:

verses.latin_vulgate: In repromissione etiam Dei non hæsitavit diffidentia, sed confortatus est fide, dans gloriam Deo:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰς δὲ τὴν ἐπαγγελίαν τοῦ θεοῦ οὐ διεκρίθη τῇ ἀπιστίᾳ ἀλλ᾽ ἐνεδυναμώθη τῇ πίστει δοὺς δόξαν τῷ θεῷ
verses.king_james: He staggered not at the promise of God through unbelief; but was strong in faith, giving glory to God;

verses.latin_vulgate: plenissime sciens, quia quæcumque promisit, potens est et facere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πληροφορηθεὶς ὅτι ὃ ἐπήγγελται δυνατός ἐστιν καὶ ποιῆσαι
verses.king_james: And being fully persuaded that, what he had promised, he was able also to perform.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ideo et reputatum est illi ad justitiam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ καὶ ἐλογίσθη αὐτῷ εἰς δικαιοσύνην
verses.king_james: And therefore it was imputed to him for righteousness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non est autem scriptum tantum propter ipsum quia reputatum est illi ad justitiam:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκ ἐγράφη δὲ δι᾽ αὐτὸν μόνον ὅτι ἐλογίσθη αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: Now it was not written for his sake alone, that it was imputed to him;

verses.latin_vulgate: sed et propter nos, quibus reputabitur credentibus in eum, qui suscitavit Jesum Christum Dominum nostrum a mortuis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ καὶ δι᾽ ἡμᾶς οἷς μέλλει λογίζεσθαι τοῖς πιστεύουσιν ἐπὶ τὸν ἐγείραντα ἰησοῦν τὸν κύριον ἡμῶν ἐκ νεκρῶν
verses.king_james: But for us also, to whom it shall be imputed, if we believe on him that raised up Jesus our Lord from the dead;

verses.latin_vulgate: qui traditus est propter delicta nostra, et resurrexit propter justificationem nostram.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς παρεδόθη διὰ τὰ παραπτώματα ἡμῶν καὶ ἠγέρθη διὰ τὴν δικαίωσιν ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: Who was delivered for our offences, and was raised again for our justification.
